data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve your current settings for subscription tracking.**

Subscription tracking adds links to the bottom of your emails that allows your recipients to subscribe to, or unsubscribe from, your emails.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current subscription tracking settings for your account. Subscription tracking adds subscribe/unsubscribe links to the bottom of your emails, allowing recipients to manage their subscription preferences. Returns the SubscriptionTrackingSettings object containing the current configuration state and enabled status.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description lacks clarity about what is returned and the specific configuration details available. Added information about the response schema (SubscriptionTrackingSettings object) and clarified the purpose for AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to enable or disable your current click tracking setting.**

Click Tracking overrides all the links and URLs in your emails and points them to either SendGrid's servers or the domain with which you branded your link. When a customer clicks a link, SendGrid tracks those [clicks](https://sendgrid.com/docs/glossary/clicks/).

Click tracking helps you understand how users are engaging with your communications. SendGrid can track up to 1000 links per email
data.description_suggestion: Enables or disables click tracking for your account. Accepts a request body with an enabled boolean flag to control whether SendGrid tracks link clicks in your emails. When enabled, all links and URLs in emails are overridden to point to SendGrid's servers or your branded domain for click tracking. Returns the updated click tracking configuration. This change applies to all future emails sent from your account.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks critical details about inputs (enabled boolean flag), outputs (returns updated configuration), and side effects (applies to all future emails). Added specifics about the request body structure and clarified that this is an account-wide setting affecting future sends.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve your current setting for Google Analytics.**


Google Analytics helps you understand how users got to your site and what they're doing there. For more information about using Google Analytics, please refer to [Google's URL Builder](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/1033867?hl=en) and their article on ["Best Practices for Campaign Building"](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/1037445).

We default the settings to Google's recommendations. For more information, see [Google Analytics Demystified](https://sendgrid.com/docs/ui/analytics-and-reporting/google-analytics/).
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves your current Google Analytics tracking configuration. Returns the Google Analytics settings object including campaign source, medium, term, content, and name parameters used to tag your email links for tracking in Google Analytics. Uses Google's recommended default values if not previously customized. This setting enables you to track email traffic and user behavior in your Google Analytics account.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and appropriate. Description provides good context about Google Analytics benefits but lacks specifics about what is returned (the actual configuration parameters). Added details about the specific fields returned and clarified the purpose of these parameters for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to update your current settings for open tracking.**

Open Tracking adds an invisible image at the end of the email which can track email opens.

If the email recipient has images enabled on their email client, a request to SendGrid's server for the invisible image is executed and an open event is logged.

These events are logged in the Statistics portal, Email Activity interface, and are reported by the Event Webhook.
data.description_suggestion: Enables or disables open tracking for your account. Accepts a request body with an enabled boolean flag to control whether SendGrid tracks email opens. When enabled, an invisible tracking image is appended to the end of each email. Open events are logged when recipients have images enabled in their email client and the image is loaded. Returns the updated open tracking configuration. Changes apply to all future emails sent from your account and events are reported in the Statistics portal, Email Activity interface, and Event Webhook.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate. Description provides good context about open tracking mechanics but lacks critical details about inputs (enabled boolean), outputs (returns updated configuration), and side effects (applies to future emails, where events are reported). Added specifics about the request structure and clarified the scope and reporting of this account-wide setting.
